classes.name: ACE inhibitors
classes.description: Angiotensin-converting-enzyme inhibitors (lisinopril, enalapril, benazepril, ramipril, etc.) -- first-line oral therapy for hypertension and heart failure.
classes.plainDescription: A common daily pill for high blood pressure or heart failure.

preferredAgent.sig: Take 1 tablet (10mg) by mouth once daily
preferredAgent.plainSig: Take one 10mg tablet by mouth once a day.

records.preferredRestriction: The lisinopril brand-name product carries a "#" marker (brand requires PA because an FDA A-rated generic equivalent exists), but the generic lisinopril tablet itself is covered without PA.

alternatives.note: Covered without PA, M90 allowed; brand requires PA per "#" marker but generic does not

paRequired.reason: True PA required (not just a brand/generic "#" marker) — MHDL Table 18 requires documentation of inadequate response or adverse reaction to two ACE inhibitors available without PA
Same PA criteria as captopril alone
Liquid formulation requires PA/medical necessity documentation distinct from the tablet
Liquid formulation requires PA per MHDL Table 18
Requires PA; MHDL notes concurrent renin-angiotensin-system therapy is evaluated case-by-case
Brand-name PA required under the "#" FDA A-rated generic equivalent rule; generic ramipril is not restricted
Brand-name PA required under the "#" FDA A-rated generic equivalent rule; generic benazepril is not restricted
Brand-name PA required under the "#" FDA A-rated generic equivalent rule; generic lisinopril is not restricted

records.stepTherapy: PA for captopril requires documentation of inadequate response or adverse reaction to two ACE inhibitors that are available without PA; requests for concurrent therapy with two or more renin-angiotensin-system agents are evaluated case-by-case.
records.tier: No explicit dollar/tier language in MHDL; generic ACE inhibitors (lisinopril, benazepril, fosinopril, moexipril, perindopril, ramipril generic, trandolapril, enalapril tablet) are covered without PA ("preferred" in practice), consistent with MassHealth's general generic-preferred policy.
records.verificationNote: Read directly from the MassHealth Drug List (MHDL) Cardiovascular Agents therapeutic table (Table 18, reached via pubtheradetail.do?id=18) and cross-checked against individual drug rows in the A-Z drug list (pubdruglist.do?index=L, index=B, index=C for lisinopril, benazepril, and captopril respectively). Confirmed: generic lisinopril, benazepril show "M90, #" (brand-only PA) while captopril and captopril/HCTZ show plain "PA" (true class-level restriction) with hyperlink to paId=21. The Cardiovascular Agents table's last-revised date read "October 2025" per the id=18 fetch; the A-Z list rows were read live in the current session (dated 2026-07-07). Preferred agent (lisinopril) and non-restricted alternatives are directly confirmed; the specific 10mg strength/sig is a standard representative dose, not verbatim from the page (the MHDL table lists PA/coverage status by drug/formulation, not detailed dosing sigs).

records.verificationNote: Read directly off the CVS Caremark-administered BCBS MA Standard Control Comprehensive Covered Medication List (effective July 2026, 'ANTIHYPERTENSIVES > ACE INHIBITORS' section) and cross-checked every brand name against the Comprehensive Drug Removal List (effective July 2026) — none of Accupril/Altace/Lotensin/Vasotec/Zestril/Prinivil appear on the removal list. This corrects the prior cell: those brands are Tier 3 covered alternatives (higher cost, not a PA/coverage barrier), not 'nonformulary' — moved from paRequired to alternatives. All 9 generic ACE inhibitors on this formulary are Tier 1 with zero PA/QL/ST anywhere in the class.

records.verificationNote: Read directly from the 'Cardiovascular Agents - Drugs for Heart and Circulation Conditions' section (PDF pages 63-67 of the fetched Tufts Health Plan Direct 3-Tier Prescription Drug List, effective Jan. 1, 2026, page footer stamped 'Last Update: 06/15/26 / Next Update: 07/15/26'). Every ACE inhibitor listed (benazepril, captopril, enalapril maleate, fosinopril, lisinopril, moexipril, perindopril erbumine, quinapril, ramipril, trandolapril) is Tier 1 with no PA/ST/QL notation; lisinopril and its HCTZ combo additionally carry a 'CRMED' flag (ConnectorCare Chronic Medications $0 copay list for select plans). No brand-only ACE inhibitor appears on the list at all (i.e., all are generic-only), confirming no BOGL and no restriction on the class. Confirmed no class-wide step-therapy language for ACE inhibitors in the glossary or drug rows.

preferredAgent.sig: Take 1 tablet (10 mg) by mouth once daily
preferredAgent.plainSig: Take one 10 mg tablet by mouth once a day

records.verificationNote: Read directly off the 'Cardiovascular Agents - Drugs for Heart and Circulation Conditions' section (PDF pages 71-72, extracted text lines ~5380-5480) of the HPHC Select 3-Tier 2026 Prescription Drug List (source PDF fetched via curl with a browser User-Agent after WebFetch returned only PDF structural code/metadata; PyMuPDF text extraction succeeded on the full 161-page document). Every ACE inhibitor generic listed alphabetically (benazepril, captopril, enalapril maleate, fosinopril sodium, lisinopril, moexipril hcl, perindopril erbumine, quinapril hcl, ramipril, trandolapril) is Tier 1 with only an 'HSA' note (HSA-preventive-drug flag) -- no PA, ST, or QL flag on any of them. No brand-name ACE inhibitor appears anywhere in the 161-page document (confirmed absent, meaning fully generic-substituted). Lisinopril was chosen as the representative preferred agent per this class's usual first-line status; the formulary itself does not single out one ACE inhibitor as 'preferred' over another -- all are equally Tier 1/unrestricted. Cover page states 'Created: October 1, 2025'; every content page carries 'Last Update: 06/15/26 / Next Update: 07/15/26' -- used 2026-06-15 as the effective date since it is the most recent timestamp shown on the actual drug-listing pages.

preferredAgent.sig: Take 1 tablet (10 mg) by mouth once daily
preferredAgent.plainSig: Take one 10 mg tablet by mouth once a day

records.verificationNote: Read directly off the MGB Health Plan 2026 6-Tier Pharmacy Formulary PDF (fm.formularynavigator.com/FBO/192/20266T.pdf, 134 pages, downloaded via curl with a browser User-Agent after confirming the domain is on the 'looks blocked but isn't' list; text extracted with PyMuPDF). The document header states 'CURRENT AS OF 7/1/2026' on every content page; PDF metadata creation/mod date is 2026-06-30. This is a flat alphabetized drug list (not sectioned by therapeutic class), so each ACE inhibitor generic was located individually: Lisinopril Oral (brand ref Zestril) = Tier 1; Benazepril HCl Oral (Lotensin) = Tier 1; Enalapril Maleate Oral Tablet (Vasotec) = Tier 1; Fosinopril Sodium = Tier 1; Quinapril HCl = Tier 1; Ramipril = Tier 1; Trandolapril = Tier 1; Captopril Oral = Tier 2; Moexipril HCl = Tier 2; Perindopril Erbumine = Tier 2. None carry a PA, ST, QL, or SP flag. No brand-name ACE inhibitor (e.g. Zestril, Vasotec) has its own separate formulary line distinct from the generic -- only the combination products (e.g. Lisinopril-hydroCHLOROthiazide/Zestoretic, Benazepril-hydroCHLOROthiazide/Lotensin HCT) are listed as combos and are out of scope here. Lisinopril was selected as the representative preferred agent per usual first-line status; the document itself does not single out one ACE inhibitor over another -- all are equally low-tier/unrestricted, so tier level (1 vs 2) is the only real distinguishing signal used to rank alternatives here.
